title: VDD static_analysis: run_shell_command executes arbitrary config strings via sh -c
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

**Dimension:** Security and Access / Perimeter Defense
**Location:** src/vdd/static_analysis.rs:32-73 (run_shell_command) + src/vdd/mod.rs:688-724 (run_static_analysis caller)
**Violation:** VddEngine::run_static_analysis pulls command strings from `self.config.static_analysis.commands` (or auto-detected from guardrails) and passes each to `sh -c <cmd>`. There is no allowlist, no shell escaping audit, and no sandboxing. If OpenClaudia ever accepts config from a less-trusted source (MCP server, remote config, imported yaml), this is RCE-by-design. Even today, a `.openclaudia/config.yaml` tampered with a malicious `vdd.static_analysis.commands: ['rm -rf $HOME']` entry runs on every VDD pass. There is no warning on `shell: sh` execution, no drop-privileges shim.
**Mandated Refactor:** (a) Parse the command string with `shlex` and execute via argv (no shell). Reject commands containing `|`, `&&`, `$( `, backticks — or document explicitly that config provenance must be trusted. (b) Add a config-time warning banner on first load listing the exact commands that will be executed. (c) Consider running under `unshare --map-root-user` or a seccomp filter on Linux.
